title: Smoky Juicy German Pepper Curry
description: A sour, juicy, indulgent German‑style curry that marries smoky tandoor heat with fresh green peppers and a tangy lemon finish.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor oven to 200 °C (392 °F) or set a heavy skillet on a 200 °C burner.
Heat the olive oil in the skillet over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds and wait until they pop.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in the ground coriander, cumin, smoked paprika, and garam masala; cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Add the sliced bell peppers and chopped tomatoes, cooking until the peppers soften, about 5–7 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, stir to coat, and reduce the heat to low.
Transfer the skillet to the tandoor (or place it in the oven) and cook for 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the peppers are tender and the sauce thickens.
Remove from heat, stir in the lemon juice and salt, and adjust seasoning with black pepper if desired.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and mint if using, and serve hot over steamed rice or warm rye bread.
